items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 15 Jul
items.summary: Trump on FOX Business: Iran wants to meet. They want to make a deal. But plans for more military operations. US military strikes Iran (again) Crude oil futures settled at $79.60 up $0.26 or 0.33% Fed Beige Book: Growth Continues, Uncertainty Lingers Iran's Qalibaf: We must always be ready to fight and stand to the end to safeguard our national security and interests Fed's Cook: It is prudent to wait a bit longer for inflation to slow, but is prepared to act White House reportedly weighing an additional extension of Jones Act waivers as renewed Iran conflict stirs price fears BOC Press conference: Q2 is looking pretty solid The Economy is looking like it is in expansion Weekly EIA crude oil inventory -1.692 million vs -2.594 million estimate Fed Chair Warsh in the Senate testifies in front of the Senate Banking Committee. Bank of Canada rate decision 2.25% vs 2.25% expected The full statement from the Bank of Canada rate decision Fed's Williams: It is imperative that we restore inflation to 2% goal on a sustained basis US Empire Manufacturing for July 15.6 vs 5.70 last month. The estimate was 8.80. May Canada manufacturing sales +1.3% vs +1.1% expected US June PPI 5.5% vs 6.2% expected Kickstart the North American session: The USD is little changed versus the major currencies. investingLive European markets wrap: Oil prices hold higher, yields push up again after US CPI breather The U.S. dollar remained under pressure following another softer-than-expected U.S. inflation report, with weaker PPI data reinforcing expectations that price pressures continue to cool and increasing the likelihood of additional Fed rate cuts by year-end. Treasury yields moved lower, dragging the greenback broadly lower across the FX market. Looking at the US yield curve: 2 year yield 4.140%, -5.2 basis points. 5 year yield 4.265%, -5.7 basis points 10 year yield 4.551%, -3.7 basis points 30 year yield 5.083%, -1.1 basis points In the forex, the GBPUSD  was the standout performer, climbing more than 1% against the dollar to a high of 1.3557 before coming off into the close to 1.3534 currently. The strongest move among the major currencies. Sterling gained not only from broad USD weakness but also from growing speculation that the UK's next Prime Minister will appoint Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood as Chancellor. Markets are interpreting the expected appointment as fiscally responsible, boosting confidence in UK assets and helping propel GBP/USD to fresh highs. Elsewhere, the EURUSD also advanced as lower U.S. yields supported the pair, while the New Zealand dollar and Australian dollar posted solid gains. Fed's Williams spoke (NY Fed Pres) and struck a moderately hawkish, but cautiously optimistic tone. He stressed that restoring inflation to the Fed's 2% target remains the central priority, saying inflation is still too high at around 4% and that current monetary policy is well positioned to continue bringing price pressures lower. He also emphasized there is no consideration of changing the Fed's 2% inflation target and said the central bank remains committed to delivering both price stability and maximum employment. Williams said there are encouraging signs that inflation has peaked, with the latest CPI report providing another indication that inflation is moving in the right direction. He expects inflation to slow to about 3.25% by year-end before gradually returning to 2% in 2028. He described the economy and labor market as resilient and stable, forecasting trend-like GDP growth and a gradual decline in unemployment. While acknowledging risks from Middle East supply disruptions and uncertainty surrounding AI-driven investment, he said the U.S. economy has so far absorbed those challenges well. Williams avoided providing any guidance on the path of interest rates, saying he has no particular view on where policy is headed and that the Fed has moved away from forward guidance. He reiterated that rates should eventually move lower once inflation is sustainably back at 2%, but not before. Overall, his comments reinforced a patient, data-dependent approach, keeping the message slightly hawkish despite growing confidence that inflation is moving in the right direction. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh largely repeated the themes from his House testimony, maintaining a mildly hawkish stance. He said recent inflation data is an imperfect gauge of underlying price pressures and stressed that inflation remains the weaker side of the Fed's dual mandate, even as the labor market is in good shape. Warsh said the Fed is prepared to adjust both interest rates and its balance sheet if needed to keep inflation under control and reiterated that the balance sheet should ultimately be as small as practical. Warsh also highlighted the surge in AI investment as a major driver of business spending and economic growth. While AI may cause one-time price increases and some job displacement, he believes it will ultimately create jobs and boost productivity. He added that he would not discuss conversations with President Trump and said the Fed's task forces are expected to begin reporting recommendations as early as September. Overall, the testimony offered little that was new and was consistent with his prior comments. The Canadian dollar strengthened modestly after the Bank of Canada left its overnight rate unchanged at 2.25%, as widely expected, while signaling increased confidence that the economy is beginning to recover after a weak start to the year. Policymakers said growth appears to have resumed in the second quarter at an annualized pace of roughly 2.5%, with the expansion broadening beyond a few sectors. While labor market conditions remain soft and housing activity has been subdued, the Bank noted signs of stabilization in housing and expects business investment to improve as companies adapt to ongoing uncertainty surrounding the USMCA trade framework. On inflation, the Bank acknowledged that headline price pressures have risen in the near term, largely due to higher gasoline prices, refinery margins, and the weaker Canadian dollar. However, it emphasized that underlying inflation remains close to the 2% target and is expected to ease gradually over time. The Governing Council reiterated that it will continue to assess incoming economic data and stands ready to adjust monetary policy if needed. Notably, the Bank removed earlier language emphasizing that it would look through the inflationary effects of geopolitical tensions, instead adopting a more balanced tone that suggests policymakers are comfortable remaining on hold while monitoring how growth and inflation evolve. The updated Monetary Policy Report (MPR) reflected this more measured outlook. The Bank downgraded its 2026 growth forecast after the economy stalled in the first quarter but lifted its projections for 2027 and 2028, indicating confidence that the recovery has been delayed rather than derailed. Overall, the statement points to a central bank that sees improving economic momentum but is in no rush to either tighten or ease policy further, with future decisions remaining firmly data dependent. In the press conference, Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem struck a cautiously optimistic tone, saying the economy appears to have regained momentum in the second quarter, with growth looking "pretty solid." While he acknowledged there are still questions about how durable the recent improvement will prove to be, he added that the Bank believes the recovery is sustainable. Macklem emphasized that policymakers will continue to evaluate incoming data before drawing firm conclusions, reinforcing that future policy decisions will be made one meeting at a time rather than following a predetermined path. On inflation, Macklem reiterated that the Bank remains vigilant. He warned that if oil prices were to rise sharply again and begin feeding more broadly into inflation, the Bank would likely need to respond with consecutive rate hikes to keep inflation under control. At the same time, he downplayed the recent weakness in the Canadian dollar, saying it has not been a major factor in the Bank's policy decisions. He noted that the softer currency has largely reflected a widening in yield differentials between Canada and the United States rather than domestic economic concerns. Overall, Macklem's comments reinforced a balanced, data-dependent approach, with the Bank seeing encouraging signs of economic improvement while remaining prepared to act if inflationary pressures re-emerge. Major currency performance vs. USD (declines for the USD): 🇬🇧 British Pound:+1.04% (best performer) 🇳🇿 New Zealand Dollar: +0.64% 🇨🇭 Swiss Franc:+0.47% (USD higher vs. CHF) 🇦🇺 Australian Dollar: +0.44% 🇪🇺 Euro: +0.38% 🇨🇦 Canadian Dollar: +0.11% 🇯🇵 Japanese Yen: +0.01% Overall, the combination of softer U.S. inflation data, falling Treasury yields, and renewed expectations for Fed easing kept the dollar on the defensive, while sterling stole the spotlight on a mix of favorable U.S. macro developments and UK political optimism. Summing up other markets, US stocks had an up and down and back up again day.  Dow industrial average rose 151.04 points or 0.29% at 52654.28 S&P rose 28.79 points or 0.38% at 7572.39 Nasdaq rose 162.22 points or 0.62% at 26269.23 Russell 2000 rose 11.49 points or 0.39% at 2976.25 In other markets: Crude oil rose $1.03 at $80.37 in an up and down day Gold rose $5 Silver fell -$1.00 Bitcoin was up $431 at $64969.  This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 特朗普在福克斯商业频道表示：伊朗希望会面。他们希望达成协议。但正计划开展更多军事行动。美军（再次）对伊朗发动空袭。原油期货收于79.60美元，上涨0.26美元，涨幅0.33%。美联储《褐皮书》：经济增长持续，不确定性犹存。伊朗的卡利巴夫：我们必须时刻做好战斗准备，并坚持到底，以捍卫我们的国家安全和利益。美联储的库克： 为等待通胀放缓再观望一段时间是审慎之举，但已做好采取行动的准备 据报道，随着伊朗冲突重燃引发价格担忧，白宫正考虑进一步延长《琼斯法案》豁免期限 加拿大央行新闻发布会：第二季度表现相当稳健 经济似乎正处于扩张阶段 美国能源信息署（EIA）每周原油库存报告：-169.2万桶，低于-259.4万桶的预期 美联储理事沃什在参议院银行委员会作证。加拿大央行利率决议：2.25%，符合市场预期2.25%。加拿大央行利率决议完整声明。美联储威廉姆斯表示：必须将通胀率持续恢复至2%的目标水平。7月美国纽约联储制造业指数为15.6，上月为5.70。市场预期为8.80。 加拿大5月制造业销售额环比增长1.3%，高于预期值1.1%；美国6月PPI为5.5%，低于预期值6.2%。北美交易时段开盘：美元兑主要货币汇率基本持平。investingLive欧洲市场综述： 油价维持高位，美国CPI数据暂缓后收益率再度走高 随着美国通胀报告再次低于预期，美元持续承压，疲软的PPI数据强化了市场对物价压力持续缓解的预期，并增加了美联储年底前进一步降息的可能性。国债收益率走低，拖累美元在外汇市场普遍走弱。观察美国收益率曲线：2年期收益率4.140%，下跌5.2个基点；5年期收益率4.265%，下跌5.7个基点；10年期收益率4.551%，下跌3.7个基点；30年期收益率5.083%，下跌1.1个基点。在外汇市场上，英镑兑美元（GBPUSD） 表现尤为突出，兑美元上涨逾1%，一度触及1.3557的高点，随后回落，目前收于1.3534附近。这是主要货币中波动最剧烈的一组。 英镑的上涨不仅得益于美元的全面走弱，还源于市场日益盛行的猜测——英国下一任首相将任命内政大臣沙巴娜·马哈茂德担任财政大臣。市场将这一预期任命解读为一种负责任的财政举措，从而提振了市场对英国资产的信心，并助推英镑兑美元汇率创下新高。此外，受美国国债收益率走低支撑，欧元兑美元汇率也出现上涨，而新西兰元和澳元则录得显著涨幅。美联储威廉姆斯（纽约联储主席）发表讲话，语气略显鹰派，但仍持谨慎乐观态度。他强调，将通胀率恢复至美联储2%的目标仍是首要任务，称当前通胀率仍处于4%左右的较高水平，而现行货币政策有能力继续缓解物价压力。他还强调，美联储并未考虑调整2%的通胀目标，并表示央行仍致力于实现物价稳定和充分就业。威廉姆斯指出，有令人鼓舞的迹象表明通胀已见顶，最新的CPI报告再次表明通胀正朝着正确方向发展。 他预计通胀率将在年底前放缓至约3.25%，随后于2028年逐步回落至2%。他将经济和劳动力市场描述为具有韧性且稳定，并预测GDP将保持趋势性增长，失业率将逐步下降。尽管承认中东供应中断带来的风险以及人工智能驱动投资带来的不确定性，但他表示，迄今为止美国经济已很好地应对了这些挑战。威廉姆斯避免就利率路径提供任何指引，称他对政策走向没有特别看法，且美联储已不再采用前瞻性指引。他重申，一旦通胀可持续地回落至2%，利率最终应下调，但在此之前不会下调。总体而言，他的言论强化了耐心、数据驱动的政策立场，尽管市场对通胀正朝着正确方向发展的信心日益增强，但其表态仍略带鹰派色彩。美联储理事凯文·沃什（Kevin Warsh）基本上重申了他在众议院作证时的观点，维持了温和鹰派的立场。 他表示，最近的通胀数据并不能完全反映潜在的价格压力，并强调即使劳动力市场状况良好，通胀仍是美联储“双重使命”中表现较弱的一环。沃什称，为控制通胀，美联储已做好必要时同时调整利率和资产负债表的准备，并重申资产负债表最终应尽可能缩减。沃什还强调，人工智能（AI）投资的激增是推动企业支出和经济增长的主要动力。尽管人工智能可能会导致一次性价格上涨和部分就业岗位被取代，但他认为这最终将创造就业机会并提高生产率。他补充说，他不会讨论与特朗普总统的谈话内容，并表示美联储的工作组预计最早将于9月开始提交建议报告。总体而言，此次证词内容鲜有新意，与他此前的表态一致。加拿大央行如市场普遍预期般将隔夜利率维持在2.25%不变，同时表示对经济在年初疲软开局后开始复苏的信心增强，受此影响，加元汇率小幅走强。 政策制定者表示，第二季度经济增长似乎已恢复，年化增速约为2.5%，且增长范围已从少数几个行业扩大到更广泛的领域。尽管劳动力市场状况依然疲软，房地产市场活动也较为低迷，但该行指出房地产市场已出现企稳迹象，并预计随着企业适应围绕《美墨加协定》（USMCA）贸易框架的持续不确定性，企业投资将有所改善。关于通胀，央行承认近期整体物价压力有所上升，这主要归因于汽油价格上涨、炼油利润率提高以及加元走弱。不过，央行强调，核心通胀率仍接近2%的目标，预计未来将逐渐缓解。管理委员会重申，将继续评估最新经济数据，并随时准备在必要时调整货币政策。值得注意的是，央行删除了此前强调将“忽略”地缘政治紧张局势对通胀影响的表述，转而采用一种更平衡的基调，这表明决策者倾向于维持现状，同时密切关注经济增长和通胀的演变。 最新发布的《货币政策报告》（MPR）反映了这种更为审慎的展望。由于第一季度经济停滞，加拿大央行下调了2026年的经济增长预测，但上调了2027年和2028年的预测，这表明央行相信经济复苏只是推迟了，而非脱轨。总体而言，该声明表明，央行虽然看到经济动能正在增强，但并不急于进一步收紧或放松政策，未来的决策仍将坚定地以数据为依据。在新闻发布会上，加拿大央行行长蒂夫·麦克莱姆（Tiff Macklem）表现得谨慎乐观，他表示经济在第二季度似乎已重拾动能，增长态势“相当稳固”。尽管他承认近期改善的可持续性仍存疑问，但他补充道，央行认为经济复苏是可持续的。麦克莱姆强调，政策制定者将在评估最新数据后再得出明确结论，并重申未来的政策决策将根据每次会议的情况逐次制定，而非遵循预先设定的路径。关于通胀，麦克莱姆重申央行仍保持警惕。 他警告称，如果油价再次大幅上涨并开始更广泛地推高通胀，央行可能需要通过连续加息来控制通胀。与此同时，他淡化了近期加元走弱的影响，称这并非央行政策决策的主要考量因素。他指出，加元走软在很大程度上反映了加拿大与美国之间的收益率差扩大，而非国内经济方面的担忧。总体而言，麦克莱姆的言论强化了央行采取平衡且以数据为导向的政策立场：央行既看到了经济改善的积极迹象，同时也做好了准备，一旦通胀压力卷土重来，将采取行动。主要货币对美元表现（美元贬值）：🇬🇧 英镑：+1.04%（表现最佳） 🇳🇿 新西兰元：+0.64% 🇨🇭 瑞士法郎：+0.47%（美元兑瑞士法郎走强） 🇦🇺 澳元：+0.44% 🇪🇺 欧元：+0.38% 🇨🇦 加元：+0.11% 🇯🇵 日元：+0.01% 总体而言，美国经济放缓与 通胀数据、国债收益率走低以及市场对美联储宽松政策的预期重燃，使美元持续承压；与此同时，受美国宏观经济利好消息与英国政治前景乐观情绪的双重提振，英镑成为市场焦点。综观其他市场，美股当天经历了一波三折的走势，最终收涨。  道琼斯工业平均指数上涨151.04点，涨幅0.29%，收于52654.28点；标普500指数上涨28.79点，涨幅0.38%，收于7572.39点；纳斯达克指数上涨162.22点，涨幅0.62%，收于26269. 23 罗素2000指数上涨11.49点，涨幅0.39%，收于2976.25点。其他市场方面：原油价格在震荡中上涨1.03美元，收于80.37美元；黄金上涨5美元；白银下跌1.00美元；比特币上涨431美元，收于64969美元。本文由investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基撰写。

Overall, the statement points to a central bank that sees improving economic momentum but is in no rush to either tighten or ease policy further, with future decisions remaining firmly data dependent. In the press conference, Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem struck a cautiously optimistic tone, saying the economy appears to have regained momentum in the second quarter, with growth looking "pretty solid." While he acknowledged there are still questions about how durable the recent improvement will prove to be, he added that the Bank believes the recovery is sustainable. Macklem emphasized that policymakers will continue to evaluate incoming data before drawing firm conclusions, reinforcing that future policy decisions will be made one meeting at a time rather than following a predetermined path. On inflation, Macklem reiterated that the Bank remains vigilant. He warned that if oil prices were to rise sharply again and begin feeding more broadly into inflation, the Bank would likely need to respond with consecutive rate hikes to keep inflation under control. At the same time, he downplayed the recent weakness in the Canadian dollar, saying it has not been a major factor in the Bank's policy decisions. He noted that the softer currency has largely reflected a widening in yield differentials between Canada and the United States rather than domestic economic concerns. Overall, Macklem's comments reinforced a balanced, data-dependent approach, with the Bank seeing encouraging signs of economic improvement while remaining prepared to act if inflationary pressures re-emerge. Major currency performance vs. USD (declines for the USD): 🇬🇧 British Pound:+1.04% (best performer) 🇳🇿 New Zealand Dollar: +0.64% 🇨🇭 Swiss Franc:+0.47% (USD higher vs. CHF) 🇦🇺 Australian Dollar: +0.44% 🇪🇺 Euro: +0.38% 🇨🇦 Canadian Dollar: +0.11% 🇯🇵 Japanese Yen: +0.01% Overall, the combination of softer U.S. inflation data, falling Treasury yields, and renewed expectations for Fed easing kept the dollar on the defensive, while sterling stole the spotlight on a mix of favorable U.S. macro developments and UK political optimism. Summing up other markets, US stocks had an up and down and back up again day.  Dow industrial average rose 151.04 points or 0.29% at 52654.28 S&P rose 28.79 points or 0.38% at 7572.39 Nasdaq rose 162.22 points or 0.62% at 26269.23 Russell 2000 rose 11.49 points or 0.39% at 2976.25 In other markets: Crude oil rose $1.03 at $80.37 in an up and down day Gold rose $5 Silver fell -$1.00 Bitcoin was up $431 at $64969.  This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive European markets wrap: Oil prices hold higher, yields push up again after US CPI breather
items.summary: Headlines: Cooler US inflation gives markets a bit of a breather USD/JPY shrugs off the surprisingly soft US CPI as Middle East crisis keeps inflation risks alive ECB policymaker Nagel says interest rates are at appropriate level after June's decision ECB policymaker Panetta says ECB's goal is to keep inflation expectations firmly anchored, limiting second-round effects ECB policymaker Stournaras says renewed US-Iran conflict reignited inflation and growth worries PBOC reaffirms to continue implementing appropriately loose monetary policy Spain headline annual inflation confirmed at 3.2% in June Euro area industrial output falls in May, missing on estimates Markets: WTI crude up 0.6% to $79.85 European indices lower, DAX down 0.8% S&P 500 futures up 0.1%, Nasdaq futures up 0.4% AUD leads, CHF lags on the day 10-year Treasury yields up 2.5 bps to 4.61% Gold down 0.5% to $4,032 Bitcoin up 0.2% to $64,652 It was a slower session with little notable headlines to work with in European morning trade. As such, markets are afforded a bit of a breather in digesting the latest US-Iran developments alongside the softer US June CPI report from yesterday. Oil prices continue to hold higher as Middle East tensions remain heightened, with WTI crude up 0.6% to $79.85 on the day. A firm break of $80 remains the next key test for traders at the moment. Meanwhile, the US dollar is recouping some losses from yesterday but the moves are relatively light so far on the day. EUR/USD is flat at 1.1418 but down from around 1.1440 earlier with USD/JPY also inching up by just 0.1% to 162.35 from around 162.00 earlier in the day. With all that is happening, the bond market is perhaps the one key spot to be wary about with long-end yields quickly climbing back up. 2-year yields in the US may be up to 4.21% only, still much lower from the high yesterday near 4.30%. However, 10-year yields in the US are now back up to 4.61% with 30-year yields pushing back up to 5.12% today. Hot, hot, hot. That could yet spill over and bite at risk sentiment some time later this week. For now though, US stocks are keeping the calm still with S&P 500 futures up 0.1% and Nasdaq futures up 0.4%. In other markets, precious metals are moving back down after the bounce yesterday with gold lower by 0.5% to $4,032 and silver down 1% to $58.03 on the day. Up next, we have the US PPI report to follow up from the CPI report yesterday. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 头条新闻：美国通胀放缓让市场稍作喘息 美元兑日元对意外疲软的美国CPI反应平淡，因中东危机使通胀风险依然存在 欧洲央行决策委员纳格尔表示，6月决议后利率已处于适当水平 欧洲央行决策委员帕内塔表示，欧洲央行的目标是稳固锚定通胀预期，限制二次影响 欧洲央行决策委员斯图纳拉斯表示，美伊 -伊朗冲突重燃了通胀和增长担忧 中国人民银行重申将继续实施适度宽松的货币政策 西班牙6月年度通胀率确认维持在3.2% 欧元区5月工业产出下滑，未达预期 市场：WTI原油上涨0.6%至79.85美元 欧洲股指走低，DAX指数下跌0.8% 标普500指数期货上涨0.1%， 纳斯达克期货上涨0.4% 澳元领涨，瑞郎垫底 10年期美债收益率上涨2.5个基点至4.61% 黄金下跌0.5%至4,032美元 比特币上涨0.2%至64,652美元 欧洲早盘交易清淡，缺乏值得关注的头条新闻。因此，市场得以稍作喘息，消化最新美伊局势进展以及昨日公布的美国6月CPI数据表现疲软。 随着中东局势持续紧张，油价继续维持在高位，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）当日上涨0.6%，报79.85美元。目前，能否坚定地突破80美元关口仍是交易员面临的下一个关键考验。与此同时，美元正在收复昨日的部分跌幅，但今日迄今为止的波动幅度相对较小。欧元兑美元持平于1.1418，但较早前的1.1440左右有所回落；美元兑日元也从盘中早些时候的162.00附近小幅上涨0.1%，至162.35。鉴于当前局势，债券市场或许是需要重点警惕的领域，因其长期债券收益率正在迅速回升。美国2年期国债收益率虽仅升至4.21%，仍远低于昨日接近4.30%的高点。然而，美国10年期国债收益率已回升至4.61%，30年期国债收益率今日更攀升至5.12%。火热、火热、火热。这可能在本周稍晚时段产生溢出效应，对风险偏好造成冲击。不过目前，美股仍保持平稳，标普500指数期货上涨0.1%，纳斯达克指数期货上涨0.4%。在其他市场方面，贵金属在昨日反弹后再度回落，黄金下跌0.5%至4,032美元，白银下跌1%至58.03美元。 接下来，我们将关注美国生产者物价指数（PPI）报告，作为对昨日消费者物价指数（CPI）报告的后续跟进。本文由investinglive.com的贾斯汀·洛（Justin Low）撰写。

items.impactZh: 关键宏观数据超预期时，点差与滑点风险通常放大，建议降低仓位并控制杠杆。
items.impactEn: When macro data surprises, spread/slippage risk often widens; consider smaller position size and tighter leverage control.

items.title: investingLive Asia-pacific FX news wrap 16 Jul
items.summary: Senior BOJ official: Delay in adjusting rates could lead to economic downturn in future US slaps a 25% tariff on some Brazil goods, exempts beef and coffee Asian chip stocks slammed as Nikkei, Kospi both slide sharply Japan finance minister repeats FX warning as yen hovers around 162 South Korean regulator to unveil single-stock leveraged ETF curbs as sidecars hit Kospi, Kosdaq BOK delivers unanimous 25bp hike to 2.75%, flags persistent inflation US completes new wave of strikes on Iran, hits Bandar Abbas PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.7909 (vs. estimate at 6.7577) Australian Consumer Inflation Expectations fall to 4.7% in July 2026 (prior 5.5%) Japan's Nikkei down 3% and South Korea's Kospi down 6% ICYMI - Saudi Arabia, UAE ask Japan to help fund Hormuz bypass pipelines Weak yen, a 40 year low, a bigger worry than BOJ hikes for many Japanese firms Kuwait and Bahrain hit as Iran fires back at fresh US strikes Trump says appreciates "gesture of Goodwill by Iran" St. Louis Fed research floats new way to measure underlying inflation Former Fed official sentenced to 38 months in prison, lied about information sharing with China ICYMI - SpaceX shares dip below IPO price for first time before paring losses UBS forecasts S&P 500 to reach 8,200 by June 2027 on strong earnings Recap - Oil edges higher as US-Iran tensions escalate, tanker disabled in Gulf Stocks end an up and down day with a move higher A fragile signal of diplomacy collides with a chip-driven rout across Asia and a decisive move from the Bank of Korea, as markets try to read whether de-escalation or deeper turmoil defines the days ahead. Summary: President Trump said on Truth Social that Iran has released an American citizen who had been wrongfully detained since December 2024, describing her only as "safely outside of Iran, and in good condition," with no further details on her identity or the release. Trump acknowledged Iran "Goodwill" Despite the release, both US and Iranian strikes continued through the day, with oil remaining supported but failing to extend recent gains Japanese and South Korean equities opened sharply weaker, with chip stocks leading losses in both markets South Korea's KOSDAQ triggered a sell-side sidecar following an earlier one on the KOSPI, as the selloff deepened South Korea's financial regulator said new measures on single-stock leveraged ETFs are coming soon, with the products increasingly linked to the scale of the selloff The Bank of Korea raised its base rate by 25bp to 2.75%, its first hike since early 2023, a move widely expected and aimed at supporting the won and anchoring inflation expectations USD/KRW fell to a two-month low following the hike, even though the move itself was fully priced in St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem spoke at an event but did not comment on the economy or policy Main article: A rare hint of a diplomatic opening did little to calm markets on Thursday, as President Trump announced that Iran has released an American citizen who had been wrongfully detained since December 2024. Writing on Truth Social, Trump said the individual, whom he did not name, is now safely outside of Iran and in good condition, offering no further detail on the circumstances of her release or the negotiations behind it. The gesture briefly raised questions about whether it signalled a thaw between Washington and Tehran, but both sides continued exchanging strikes through the day, and oil, while remaining supported, did not push further to the upside. The bigger story for markets was a sharp, chip-led selloff across Asia. Japan's Nikkei and South Korea's Kospi both opened sharply weaker, with memory and equipment makers including Kioxia, SoftBank Group and Advantest leading Tokyo's declines, while SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics dragged the Kospi down hard enough to trigger a sell-side sidecar. The selling later spread to the KOSDAQ, which triggered its own sidecar following the Kospi's. South Korea's Financial Services Commission chief said new measures on single-stock leveraged ETFs would be announced soon, a signal directly tied to the growing scrutiny of those products' role in amplifying the day's swings. Against that backdrop, the Bank of Korea delivered a widely expected 25 basis point hike, lifting its base rate to 2.75% in its first move since early 2023. The decision, backed by all but one of 37 economists polled by Reuters, was aimed squarely at shoring up a depreciating won and keeping a lid on stubborn inflation expectations. Despite the hike being fully priced in, USD/KRW still slipped to a two-month low in its wake, suggesting the currency reaction ran somewhat ahead of what the consensus call alone would imply. Elsewhere, St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem made an appearance at an event but offered no comment on the economy or monetary policy, leaving his recent hawkish signals on inflation as the most current read on his thinking heading into the next stretch of Fed commentary. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 日本央行高级官员：推迟调整利率可能导致未来经济下滑 美国对部分巴西商品加征25%关税，牛肉和咖啡获豁免 亚洲芯片股重挫，日经指数和韩国KOSPI指数双双大幅下挫 日本财务大臣重申外汇警告，日元汇率徘徊在162左右 韩国监管机构将公布针对个股杠杆ETF的限制措施，因“侧车”交易冲击KOSPI指数， KOSDAQ指数 韩国央行一致决定加息25个基点至2.75%，并指出通胀持续存在 美国对伊朗发动新一轮空袭，打击班达尔阿巴斯港 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.7909（市场预期为6.7577） 2026年7月澳大利亚消费者通胀预期降至4.7%（前值5.5%） 日本日经指数下跌3%，韩国KOSPI指数下跌6% 值得关注——沙特阿拉伯和阿联酋请求日本协助资助霍尔木兹海峡绕行管道项目 日元贬值至40年低点，对许多日本企业而言比日本央行加息更令人担忧 伊朗对美国新一轮空袭进行反击，科威特和巴林受波及 特朗普表示赞赏“伊朗的善意姿态” 圣…… 圣路易斯联储研究提出衡量核心通胀的新方法 前美联储官员因就向中国共享信息一事撒谎被判处38个月监禁 错过了吗？——SpaceX股价首次跌破IPO发行价，随后收窄跌幅 瑞银预测，受强劲财报推动，标普500指数将于2027年6月升至8,200点 市场回顾 - 随着美伊紧张局势升级，一艘油轮在海湾地区遭遇故障，油价小幅走高 股市经历震荡后收高 一丝脆弱的外交信号与亚洲地区由芯片股引发的抛售潮以及韩国央行果断举措形成碰撞，市场正试图判断未来几天是局势缓和还是动荡加剧。摘要：特朗普总统在“Truth Social”平台上表示，伊朗已释放一名自2024年12月以来被错误拘留的美国公民，仅称其“已安全离开伊朗，身体状况良好”，但未透露其身份或获释的进一步细节。 特朗普承认伊朗的“善意”。尽管伊朗已释放人质，但美伊双方的空袭在当天持续进行，油价虽仍获支撑，但未能延续近期涨势。日本和韩国股市开盘大幅下挫，两地市场中芯片股领跌。继韩国KOSPI指数早前触发卖方侧车机制后，韩国KOSDAQ指数也随之触发该机制， 随着抛售势头加剧，韩国金融监管机构表示将很快出台针对个股杠杆ETF的新措施，因这些产品与抛售规模的关联度日益加深。韩国央行将基准利率上调25个基点至2.75%，这是自2023年初以来的首次加息， 此举符合市场普遍预期，旨在支撑韩元并稳定通胀预期。尽管加息本身已被市场充分消化，但美元兑韩元汇率仍跌至两个月低点。圣路易斯联储主席阿尔贝托·穆萨莱姆在某活动上发表讲话，但未就经济或政策发表评论。主条目： 周四，尽管特朗普总统宣布伊朗已释放一名自2024年12月以来被错误拘留的美国公民，这一罕见的外交缓和信号仍未能平息市场波动。 特朗普在Truth Social上发文称，这名未透露姓名的女子目前已安全离开伊朗，身体状况良好，但他并未透露有关其获释的具体情况或背后的谈判细节。这一举动曾短暂引发外界质疑，即这是否预示着华盛顿与德黑兰之间的关系出现缓和，但双方当天仍持续互发袭击，原油价格虽仍获支撑，但未能进一步上涨。对市场而言，更引人关注的是亚洲市场由芯片股领跌的剧烈抛售。日本日经指数和韩国KOSPI指数开盘均大幅下挫，以铠侠、软银集团和爱德万测试为代表的存储器及设备制造商领跌东京股市，而SK海力士和三星电子则拖累KOSPI指数大幅下挫，甚至触发了卖方“侧车”机制。随后抛售潮蔓延至KOSDAQ市场，继KOSPI之后，该市场也触发了自身的“侧车”机制。韩国金融服务委员会主席表示，将很快公布针对个股杠杆ETF的新措施，这一信号直接与市场对这些产品在放大当日波动中的作用日益加剧的审查有关。 在此背景下，韩国银行如市场普遍预期般将基准利率上调25个基点，将其基准利率上调至2.75%，这是该行自2023年初以来的首次加息。这一决定得到了路透社调查的37位经济学家中除一人以外所有人的支持，其目的正是为了支撑持续贬值的韩元，并抑制顽固的通胀预期。尽管此次加息已被市场完全消化，但美元兑韩元汇率在加息后仍跌至两个月低点，这表明汇市反应在某种程度上超出了市场共识的预期。此外，圣路易斯联储主席阿尔贝托·穆萨莱姆（Alberto Musalem）出席了一场活动，但未就经济或货币政策发表评论，因此他近期关于通胀的鹰派信号，成为解读其在下一轮美联储发言前最新立场的主要依据。本文作者为investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹（Eamonn Sheridan）。
